module.name: Search
module.param: | rest /servicesNS/-/-/configs/conf-app splunk_server=local | rename eai:acl.app as app | search app="$sideview.currentApp$" | stats last(build) as build last(version) as version last(description) as description | appendcols [ | rest /services/apps/local splunk_server=local | search title="cisco_cdr" | stats count | eval cisco_cdr_installed=if("$test_cisco_cdr_not_installed$_foo"=="1_foo" OR count=0,"cisco_cdr_not_installed","cisco_cdr_is_installed") | table cisco_cdr_installed ]

module.name: HTML
module.param:
How do we use this app?

Start with the setup documentation for this app if you haven't already. One spoiler is that the actual ingestion of your Webex data ends up being handled by a separate TA developed by Cisco.

This app then serves as an adapter, allowing the webex data to be pulled into Sideview's commercial app Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics.

In practical terms that means you and your team can investigate and chart your way through the webex data in a sophisticated user interface, covering a much wider range of functionality, but without ever having to learn or even see the Splunk search language, aka SPL.

module.name: HTML
module.param:
The Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app is not currently installed here, but you can learn more about it on our website. It is available as a free trial and can be set up quite easily following these steps.

No matter your situation, hopefully you are talking to us and you can always reach us at info@sideviewapps.com.

module.name: HTML
module.param: (hit Escape to close, or click the little X at the top right here).

module.name: HTML
module.param: Welcome to Sideview's Supporting App for Webex $version$

module.name: Search
module.param: | rest /servicesNS/-/-/configs/conf-checklist splunk_server=local | rename eai:appName as app search as searchToRun | search app="$sideview.currentApp$" | fields app title searchToRun drilldown | search title="critical_*" | eval drilldownLabel=if(isnull(drilldown),"see health check results","run troubleshooting search") | eval drilldown=if(isnull(drilldown),searchToRun,drilldown)

module.name: HTML
module.param: One or more critical health checks have failed and should be addressed as soon as possible:

module.name: HTML
module.param: If you need more detail or help fixing these issues, contact us at support@sideviewapps.com

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` (sourcetype="cisco:webex:call:detailed_history" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meeting:qualities" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meetings") | eval clientType = lower(clientType) + if(match(lower(clientType),"_client"), "s", "_clients") | eval {type}_call_id = call_id | eval {clientType}_call_id = call_id ``` hardwareType serverRegion ``` | eval {activity}_call_id = call_id | eval osType = lower(osType) + "_os" | eval {osType}_call_id = call_id | fields *_call_id call_id | stats dc(call_call_id) as calls dc(meeting_call_id) as meetings dc(*_call_id) as * | fields calls meetings *_clients *_os * | rename total as "total calls"

module.name: HTML
module.param: WARNING: The Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app is not currently installed. Drilldowns on this page will only include links to the generic Splunk Search UI using raw SPL.

module.name: Link
module.param: Investigate these calls in the Cisco CDR Reporting and Analytics app

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` (sourcetype="cisco:webex:call:detailed_history" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meeting:qualities" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meetings") | eval endTime = _time + duration | stats min(_time) as _time max(endTime) as end values(type) as type by call_id | eval type = type + "s" | eval duration=end - _time ``` inline version of the Cisco CDR apps get_concurrency macro, the complex version with a split-by ``` | eval increment = if(duration>0,mvappend("1","-1"),"0") | mvexpand type | mvexpand increment | eval _time = if(increment==1, _time, _time + duration) | sort 0 + _time | fillnull type value="NULL" | streamstats sum(increment) as post_concurrency by type | eval concurrency = if(increment==-1, post_concurrency+1, post_concurrency) ``` inline version of the Cisco CDR apps timechart_for_concurrency macro, the complex version with split-by and bins``` | timechart fixedrange=false bins=400 max(concurrency) as max_concurrency last(post_concurrency) as last_concurrency by type limit=60 | filldown last_concurrency* | foreach "max_concurrency:*" [eval <<MATCHSTR>>=coalesce('max_concurrency:<<MATCHSTR>>','last_concurrency:<<MATCHSTR>>')] | fields - last_concurrency* max_concurrency* | fillnull

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` (sourcetype="cisco:webex:call:detailed_history" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meeting:qualities" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meetings") | chart dc(call_id) as calls over callingPartyName | sort 0 calls desc | head 20

module.name: HTML
module.param: chart calls with $chart.xValue$ over time (cisco_cdr not installed)

module.name: Link
module.param: view raw CSV events in Splunk

module.name: Search
module.param: `custom_index` (sourcetype="cisco:webex:call:detailed_history" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meeting:qualities" OR sourcetype="cisco:webex:meetings") | chart dc(call_id) over hour_of_day by day_of_week

module.name: HTML
module.param: chart top callingPartyNames for $chart.searchTerms$ (cisco_cdr not installed)

module.name: Link
module.param: view raw CSV events in Splunk
